@mdavidallen Yeah but if I take your $10 and put it in my special box and tell everyone the box is worth $100, when everyone realizes the box is worthless, do you get your $10 back? Do I keep it? Does it get all burnt up?
“I want things to be better” and “I don’t want things to change” are mutually exclusive, as explained by a million pieces of folklore

So I notice, in the ever growing chorus of Musk-criticizers, what they are defending in their criticism. You can very easily criticize the intention and effects, but a lot of people regress to just criticizing a change of the status quo. Little worldview slips.
“It has always been like X” and “I like X how it is now” have never been good reasons to not pursue Y – it is lazy and only perpetuates broken systems, yuck
Gotta be careful to not accidentally criticize your enemies for rocking the status quo- the status quo needs no defenders, any reasonable criticism is deeper than that

commits.message: refactor: clean up TOC spacing and layout
fix: add padding to project content to prevent tag overlap
feat(nvim): override snacks_picker LSP bindings with native LSP
fix: remove duplicate project titles
refactor: clean up projects data structure, remove duplicates
fix: remove unused variables from components
feat: Kalshi calibration + code quality refactoring
refactor: code quality improvements and LOC reduction
docs: document Nov 15 2025 session - nvim transparency, CIPHER coach, security cleanup
chore: remove secrets from .bash_profile, improve .gitignore
fix(sketchybar): restore CIPHER coach and next_event features
chore: remove claude.json from git tracking
feat(nvim,sketchybar): transparent editor bg + battery fade + next event
refactor(nvim-theme): improve vulpes-reddishnovember-dark readability
refactor(tmux): remove sesh sessionizer - doesn't fit pane-based workflow
docs(tmux): document 2025 workflows in CLAUDE.md memory
feat(tmux): add 2025 modern workflows - sesh sessionizer, popups, .t files
chore: Consolidate scripts and remove cruft
Merge branch 'feature/observational-learning'
fix: Update observation-handler to use correct OpenRouterService API
feat: Add discord-send-message capability with guild whitelist security
refactor(tmux): reorganize config into logical sections + add pane capture bindings
fix: Improve rebuild script with proper stop/build/start sequence and polling
add: Simple local rebuild script for services
fix: Simplify loop decision logic - execute loop if capabilities are detected
fix: Auto-enable ReAct loop when LLM generates capability tags
fix: Add wants_loop tag instruction to capability prompts
Remove dead code from abandoned bundle autosave feature
feat: Add comprehensive tech support capabilities suite for Discord/GitHub integration
feat: Add general-purpose discord-channels capability for querying any Discord channel
Optimize GeoJSON generation with color object caching
Fix map performance degradation with stable color memoization
Add neomutt, wezterm, and alacritty exporters
remove: Delete Subway Builder-specific support prompt
Fix critical autosave bugs from beta tester reports
feat: Add Subway Builder beta support system with minimal LLM-driven issue management
Fix: Stop Discord RPC error spam + smart corrupted file cleanup
Merge pull request #246 from colindm/feature/autosave-indicator
Merge branch 'beta' into feature/autosave-indicator
CRITICAL FIX: Stop auto-deleting saves with parse errors
Fix: Save-and-quit flow actually quits after saving
feat: Add bot-controlled loop decision making
